---
title: "My Portfolio Website Project"
subtitle: "Proposal Presentation"
author: "Ari Nurhadi"
institute: "IBM 3202, Cal Poly Pomona"
date: "Today"
format: 
  revealjs:
    theme: serif
    width: 1600
    height: 900
    footer: My Portfolio Website Project
    transition: slide
    transition-speed: default
    incremental: false
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 1
    slide-number: true
    scrollable: true
    smaller: true
    code-fold: false
    code-overflow: wrap
    number-sections: false
    number-depth: 5
    embed-resources: true
    css: styles.css
editor: visual
execute: 
  echo: true
  freeze: auto
---

# My Portfolio Website Project

**Proposal Presentation**

**Ari Nurhadi**\
**IBM 3202, Cal Poly Pomona**

::: notes
Introduce the presentation as a proposal for your portfolio website project. Explain that the website will be designed to showcase your data analysis work, projects, dashboards, and presentations.
:::

# Project Overview

**What is this website project?**

This project is a proposal for building a professional portfolio website that can be used to present my academic work, technical skills, and data projects in a clear and organized way.

**Main purpose**

-   Showcase my work in one place
-   Build a professional online presence
-   Support internship and job applications
-   Demonstrate skills in data analysis and communication

::: notes
Explain that this website is both an academic project and a professional portfolio that can be useful after graduation.
:::

# Why I Want to Build This Website

**Goals of the project**

::: incremental
-   Organize my course projects and assignments
-   Present my work more professionally
-   Share my dashboards, reports, and presentations
-   Create a portfolio that grows over time
:::

> The website will serve as a digital portfolio that shows both technical skills and communication skills.

# Planned Website Structure

**Required pages**

::::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Core Pages**

-   Home Page
-   Marketing Projects Page
-   Dashboard Page
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
**Additional Pages**

-   Revealjs Presentation Page
-   Analytics Page
-   Future project pages
:::
:::::

::: notes
Briefly explain that the structure follows the syllabus requirements and is designed to make the website easy to navigate.
:::

# Home Page

**Purpose of the home page**

The home page will introduce who I am, what I am studying, and what visitors can expect from the website.

**Planned content**

-   Short personal introduction
-   Professional interests
-   Overview of skills
-   Navigation to major pages

This page will function as the entry point to the rest of the portfolio.

# Marketing Projects Page

**What will appear on this page?**

The projects page will showcase my work in data analysis and marketing-related problem solving.

**Example content**

-   Course assignments
-   Data visualizations
-   Case studies
-   Written reports
-   Project summaries with findings

::: aside
This page can contain multiple projects, which makes it one of the most important parts of the website.
:::

# Dashboard Page

**Purpose of the dashboard page**

The dashboard page will present key insights from data in a visual and interactive format.

**Planned features**

-   Summary charts
-   Key performance indicators
-   Interactive or embedded dashboards
-   Clear explanations of the metrics

The dashboard page will help demonstrate my ability to communicate findings visually.

# Revealjs Presentation Page

**Why include a Revealjs presentation page?**

The website will include a page for Revealjs presentations so I can share slide-based work directly on the web.

**Benefits**

-   Present ideas in a structured format
-   Share presentations through a link
-   Combine storytelling, visuals, and analysis
-   Build experience with web-based presentations

# Analytics Page

**Purpose of the analytics page**

The analytics page will focus on website traffic and user behavior data.

**Planned components**

-   Google Analytics 4 connection
-   Google Search Console integration
-   BigQuery data access
-   Looker Studio visualizations
-   Quarto report with insights

This page will help demonstrate analytical thinking by showing how website performance data can be interpreted and communicated.

# Tools and Technology

**What I plan to use**

-   Quarto
-   Revealjs
-   GitHub Pages or Netlify
-   R and Tidyverse
-   Google Analytics 4
-   Looker Studio
-   CSS for styling

::: notes
Explain that these tools support both technical work and publishing, which makes the final website more professional and useful.
:::

# Why This Project Matters

**Career and learning benefits**

::: incremental
-   Builds a professional online portfolio
-   Makes projects easier to share with others
-   Demonstrates data and communication skills
-   Connects coursework to career preparation
:::

Overall, this project is valuable because it combines technical skills, storytelling, and professional presentation in one place.

# Next Steps

**What I will do next**

1.  Create the basic website structure
2.  Add project pages and content
3.  Build and publish a Revealjs presentation
4.  Develop analytics and dashboard components
5.  Continue improving the site over time

Thank you!
